title: centaurea spina-badia subsp. shuttleworthii
intro: Centaurea spina-badia subsp. shuttleworthii, also known as Shuttleworth's knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and fight off infections. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Centaurea spina-badia subsp. shuttleworthii can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
